events.name: BranchCreated
events.trigger: ProvisionBranch action completed. Fires exactly once per new `branches.id`.
consumers: branch::MaybeDispatchGeocodeAddressJob (fires only when tenant has branch_geocoding entitlement)

events.name: BranchUpdated
events.trigger: Any mutable field on Branch was updated. Distinct from the specific-field events below (BranchAddressUpdated, BranchCoordinatesUpdated, …) — this one fires for everything else (name, description, phone, email, website, sort_order, logo_url).

events.name: BranchStatusChanged
events.trigger: Branch.status transitioned to any new value. A superset event; the specific-state events below fire alongside for the routing case that cares.

events.name: BranchPaused
events.trigger: Branch.status transitioned active → paused. New bookings + registrations refused via the enforce_operating_hours middleware.
consumers: notifications::DispatchBranchOpeningHoursChangedNotification (paused counts as a hours change to members with active memberships)

events.name: BranchClosed
events.trigger: Branch.status transitioned to closed. Refused when active downstream rows reference the branch — this event only fires after that check passes.

payload.successor_branch_id: ULID | null (when the tenant designated a replacement venue)

events.name: BranchReopened
events.trigger: Branch.status transitioned closed → active. Refused when a successor branch was created under the same (tenant, organization) after the close event.

events.name: BranchDefaultChanged
events.trigger: The default branch for a (tenant, organization) tuple was reassigned. Fires with both the newly-default row + the previously-default row so downstream caches can drop keys keyed on both.

events.name: BranchOpeningHoursUpdated
events.trigger: Branch.opening_hours was updated. Distinct from BranchUpdated because the notification consumers care about member-facing hours changes specifically.
consumers: notifications::MaybeDispatchBranchOpeningHoursChangedNotification (to members with active memberships referencing this branch, opt-in)

events.name: BranchCapacityChanged
events.trigger: Branch.capacity was updated (set, changed, or cleared).

events.name: BranchCoordinatesUpdated
events.trigger: Branch.coordinates was set OR changed. Distinct from BranchUpdated because the Wave 3 geofencing module subscribes to this event to recompute polygon coverage for every geofence attached to this Branch.
consumers: geofencing::RecomputeGeofenceCoverageJob (Wave 3, when the geofencing module lands)

events.name: BranchAddressUpdated
events.trigger: Branch.address was updated. Distinct from BranchUpdated because it optionally triggers a re-geocode when the tenant has `branch_geocoding` entitlement + coordinates.source === 'geocoded' (i.e. the coordinates were auto-derived; a manual pin drop stays pinned).

events.name: BranchDeleted
events.trigger: Hard-delete completed. Wave 2 refuses public hard-delete (closed is terminal); this event only fires on the two-person platform-support CLI path.
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (retention: 7 years — the audit outlives the row)

payload.branch_id: ULID (the row is gone; the ULID is preserved in the audit trail)
payload.cascade_summary: { facilities: int, teams: int, sessions: int, bookings: int } — for post-mortem
